name: KANO Ontology (Kano Model Feature Classification)
description: Classifies product/service features by customer satisfaction response using the Kano Model (Noriaki Kano, 1984). Provides survey instruments, functional/dysfunctional question pairs, statistical feature classification (Must-Be, Performance, Attractive, Indifferent, Reverse), non-linear satisfaction curve modelling, temporal decay tracking, and aggregated feature prioritisation. Operates as a parallel analytical lens enriching the VP-to-PMF bridge. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SA sub-series.

components.KanoSurvey: Complete Kano survey instrument targeting a customer segment with functional/dysfunctional question pairs
components.KanoQuestion: Functional/dysfunctional question pair for evaluating a single product feature
components.KanoClassification: Feature-to-category assignment with statistical confidence from cross-tabulation analysis
components.SatisfactionCurve: Non-linear satisfaction function model per Kano category (Asymptotic, Linear, Exponential, Flat, Inverse)
components.KanoDecay: Temporal category migration tracking — detects feature commoditisation over time
components.FeaturePriority: Aggregated prioritisation output with investment recommendation derived from Kano evidence

crossOntology.name: classifiedBy
crossOntology.purpose: VP features are classified by Kano analysis for satisfaction attribution

crossOntology.name: informsValidation
crossOntology.purpose: Kano classification evidence feeds PMF validation signals

crossOntology.name: triggersAssessment
crossOntology.purpose: Category decay triggers PMF pivot assessment for strategic response

crossOntology.name: measuredBy
crossOntology.purpose: Satisfaction curves are measured by KPIs for continuous monitoring

crossOntology.name: assignedFramework
crossOntology.purpose: MECE decomposition assigns Kano framework for feature analysis

domain: Customer Satisfaction Analysis, Feature Classification, Product Strategy

versionHistory.changes: Initial release — Kano Model feature classification: surveys, question pairs, cross-tabulation classification, satisfaction curves, decay tracking, feature prioritisation

notes: Layer 6 (Parallel Analytical Lens) of VSOM Strategy Analysis. Same pattern as L6S/DMAIC in PE-Series. Enriches VP-to-PMF bridge with feature-level satisfaction attribution. Not a chain link — operates as a side-analysis overlay. 6 entities, 7 business rules, 5 join patterns, 8 competency questions.
